package handler

import (
	"context"
	"database/sql"
	"errors"
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for transaction_error_translation_done_in_repository_and_handler.
// Rule intent: Both repository and handler layers translating the same transaction errors.
type Order struct {
	ID     string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
	Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
}

type OrderRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

func (r *OrderRepository) FindByID(ctx context.Context, id string) (*Order, error) {
	var order Order
	err := r.db.WithContext(ctx).Where("id = ?", id).First(&order).Error
	if err != nil {
		if errors.Is(err, gorm.ErrRecordNotFound) {
			return nil, errors.New("order not found")
		}
		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrConnDone) {
			return nil, errors.New("database connection closed")
		}
		return nil, errors.New("failed to fetch order")
	}
	return &order, nil
}

func PositiveTransactionErrorTranslationDoneInRepositoryAndHandler(c *gin.Context, repo *OrderRepository, orderID string) {
	order, err := repo.FindByID(c.Request.Context(), orderID)
	if err != nil {
		if errors.Is(err, gorm.ErrRecordNotFound) {
			c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, gin.H{"error": "order not found"})
			return
		}
		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrConnDone) {
			c.JSON(http.StatusServiceUnavailable, gin.H{"error": "database unavailable"})
			return
		}
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "internal server error"})
		return
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, order)
}
